content.full_text:
March 20, 1981


Mike Roos
California Legislature
State Capitol, Room 4116
Sacramento, California

Dear Assemblyman Roos:

Ted Prim, Executive Director of the Fair Political Practices
Commission, has asked me to reply to your letter dated February 25,
1981, regarding the Ferraro-Roos Renter Advisory Committee.

Because the committee is not being formed for the purpose of
influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for
or against the nomination or election of a candidate or the passage
or defeat of any measure, the renter advisory committee you describe
would not be a "committee" within the definition of the Political
Reform Act, and would not be required to register with the Secretary
of State or file campaign statements.

If, however, the Renters Advisory Committee ever does engage
in political activities it would be required to register and file
campaign statements once it has met any of the criteria in Gov. Code
Section 82013 for qualification of committees.

I am unaware of any governmental agency which regulates the
type of activities the renters advisory committee will be participa-
ting in.

Please contact me if you have any questions.

Sincerely,


Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA
(916) 445-7644

DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESS
6150 NEW HAMPSHIRE AVE
LONG BEACH, CALIFORNIA
(213) 366-6048

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEES:
VICE-CHAIRMAN
HOUSING AND COMMUNITY
DEVELOPMENT
CRIMINAL JUSTICE
WAYS AND MEANS

SUBCOMMITTEES:
CHAIRMAN
HOUSING PRODUCTION
VICE-CHAIRMAN
JOINT COMMITTEE ON PEACE
OFFICER CLASSIFICATION
WAYS AND MEANS
SUBCOMMITTEE 4—STATE
ADMINISTRATION
SELECT COMMITTEE ON THE
REVISION OF THE NONPROFIT
CORPORATIONS CODE

MIKE ROOS
ASSEMBLYMAN, FORTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

February 25, 1981

Mr. Ted Prim, Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Prim:

Last year Los Angeles City Council President John Ferraro and I started the Ferraro/Roos Renter Advisory Committee for our mutual constituents. Presently, about 6,000 renters belong to the Committee. We solicit their views on landlord/tenant issues, keep them informed of changes in State and city laws and operate a recorded phone message which is updated every two weeks with renter information. The number has been disseminated to the general public.

Both Councilman Ferraro and I have shared the costs of printing and mailing from our campaign funds. However, as the number of renters who join our Committee has grown so has the costs to our campaign funds. Therefore, we would like to make the Committee self-financing.

Our plan is to ask each member of the Committee for donations of $1 or $2 dollars through a mail solicitation. The donations from the letter would be used to help defray the costs of the Renter Advisory Committee, and no funds would be used by the campaigns of either myself or Councilman Ferraro or any other political campaign. All Committee members would receive a yearly accounting of how the funds collected were used and whether a deficit or surplus remains. A separate bank account under the Committee name would be established.

Mr. Ted Prim
Page 2


We would, however, like your opinion on the best way to proceed.
Is it necessary that the Renter Advisory Committee file with the
PPPC as a separate committee with its own ID number?  Or may we
simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin
soliciting?  If PPPC requirements do not regulate such an
organization are there other reporting requirements with any other
agency?

If you have questions about this request, please call Jim McDermott
in my district office at (213) 386-8042.  Thank you for your
assistance.


                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        MIKE ROOS,
                                        Majority Floor Leader

MR:bjc

sections.question: Is it necessary that the Renter Advisory Committee file with the FPPC as a separate committee with its own ID number? Or may we simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin soliciting? If FPPC requirements do not regulate such an organization are there reporting requirements with any other agency?
sections.question_synthetic: Assemblyman Mike Roos and LA City Council President John Ferraro seek guidance on whether their jointly-operated Ferraro/Roos Renter Advisory Committee must register with the FPPC as a separate committee if they solicit member donations to make it self-financing, and whether other agencies have jurisdiction.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The document contains a clear question section but no conclusion/advice from FPPC. Document quality is poor (OCR artifacts present) but key content is extractable. No FPPC letterhead or response language present.

Or may we simply open a separate bank account for the Committee and begin soliciting? If FPPC requirements do not regulate such an organization are there reporting requirements with any other agency?

embedding.summary: Assemblyman Mike Roos requests FPPC guidance on the regulatory and filing requirements for the Ferraro/Roos Renter Advisory Committee, a joint project with LA City Council President John Ferraro that currently has 6,000 members and is funded through campaign funds but seeks to become self-financing through member donations.
